title: Cinematic Content for Italian Eatery
skills: CONTENT CREATION | Content Creation | Cinematography | Social Media Management | Video Editing | After Effects | Videography | Video Production
full_description:
I run a cosy Italian restaurant in Oxley Park, Sydney and want to bring the warmth of our kitchen to Instagram, Facebook and TikTok. I’m after a single creative who can both capture and craft the visuals—think rich, cinematic shots of handmade pasta being rolled, wood-fired pizzas sliding from the oven, and the lively atmosphere of a Friday night service—and then turn that footage into scroll-stopping social posts.

You’ll need to come on-site regularly, film and photograph both food and ambience in vertical format, edit the footage into short Reels/TikToks as well as a few high-quality stills, and supply caption ideas that keep our tone conversational and authentic.

Deliverables 
• 4–6 vertical videos (15–45 sec) per visit, graded and sound-tracked for a cinematic feel 
• 10+ edited photos ready for feed and story use 
• Captions/hashtag suggestions tailored to each platform 
• All raw files supplied via cloud link 

Please share a link to similar restaurant or food work you’ve shot in a cinematic style, along with the gear you like to use. I’m keen to get our first shoot scheduled this month.

title: AI Reel Generator Website
skills: AI CONTENT CREATION | AI Content Creation | AI Video | API | AI Development | Python | PHP | JavaScript | HTML | AI Model Development | AI Text-to-video
full_description:
I need a bespoke web-based system that can automatically create and publish faceless, entertainment-style short reels directly to YouTube Shorts, TikTok and IG. The flow I picture is simple for the end user yet technically tight under the hood: a visitor enters or selects a topic, the site pulls or generates script-ready material, stitches visuals together without showing any on-camera personality, adds music or sound effects, and then pushes the finished 60-second clip to my linked YouTube channel, Tiktok and IG via the API.

Key functions I expect 
• AI-driven content generation (script, voice, music and footage) tuned for entertainment niches 
• Automatic video assembly and aspect-ratio formatting for Shorts 
• One-click or scheduled publishing with title, description, and hashtag handling 
• A lightweight dashboard so I can review, approve, or edit clips before they go live

I have no fixed opinion on the exact tech stack as long as it scales and stays maintainable; TensorFlow/PyTorch, ffmpeg, Node/React, Python-based backends, or comparable tools are all acceptable if you justify the choice. Clean, well-commented code and deployment documentation are must-haves so I can keep the project running without hand-holding.

To help me gauge fit, please link to past work that shows you have built AI or automation pipelines for video, social publishing platforms, or similar complex web apps. Screenshots or short Loom walkthroughs are fine if the projects are private.

Once delivered I’ll test the site by generating and scheduling a week’s worth of Shorts. A smooth run without manual intervention will mark acceptance.

title: Sales-Focused Facebook Ad Campaign
skills: COPYWRITING | Copywriting | Internet Marketing | Facebook Marketing | Advertising | Social Media Marketing | Conversion Rate Optimization | Digital Marketing | Facebook Ads
full_description:
I want a concise, conversion-oriented Facebook ad campaign crafted for our security camera line. The single objective is sales, so every element—from headline to call-to-action—must be tuned for purchase intent rather than pure awareness or lead capture.

My market spans both homeowners who want peace of mind and businesses that need around-the-clock monitoring, so messaging should speak naturally to each segment without duplicating effort. The standout benefit I need emphasized is our high-quality video resolution; it’s the feature that closes deals for us. Price and installation ease can be hinted at only if it helps reinforce value, but they are not the hero message.

Deliverables I’m expecting:
• 3–4 primary ad variations (copy + suggested imagery or video concept) optimised for Facebook Feed and Story placements 
• A brief rationale for each variation explaining why it should convert 
• Suggested targeting tweaks or audience splits to test homeowner vs. business angles 
• Basic performance benchmarks to watch once the ads go live

I’ll provide brand guidelines, product photos and specs as soon as we kick off. Please share any past Facebook ads you’ve written—especially those that drove direct sales—so I can see your conversion focus in action.

title: CreatiQ - Web Application for Indian Creators
skills: WEBSITE DESIGN | Website Design | Web Development | Web Services
full_description:
Project title
Build CreatiQ — a web app for Indian creators to track brand deals, earnings, payment status, and export tax-ready CSVs.
Project overview
CreatiQ is a SaaS web app for Indian creators who earn from multiple income sources such as Instagram, YouTube, direct brand deals, UGC work, and affiliate programs. The app helps creators track deals in one dashboard, monitor payment status, understand earnings trends, and export clean CSV files for tax filing, GST, ITR, and accounting use �.
Today, many creators track earnings across WhatsApp, Google Sheets, Notion, email, and memory, which creates confusion, missed payments, and tax-time chaos. CreatiQ solves this with a simple dashboard-first system designed specifically for Indian creators, with a free tier for up to 5 deals and a paid tier at ₹99/month for unlimited tracking and analytics �.
Who this product is for
Primary users:
Indian creators aged roughly 18–35.
Follower range: approximately 5K–100K.
Creator types: UGC creators, Instagram creators, YouTubers, affiliate marketers, freelance creators, and small creator agencies.
Monthly creator income: roughly ₹25K–₹3L.
Not for:
Mega influencers with very few deals.
Brands or influencer marketing teams.
Non-creators or salaried workers.
Main problem to solve
Creators earn from multiple channels but their income records are scattered. They often do not know:
How much they earned this month.
Which brands have paid and which have not.
Which platform or content type earns best.
What to send their CA/accountant for taxes.
This is a real and growing pain point as creator-income tools increasingly focus on unified tracking, exports, and visibility rather than ad hoc spreadsheets �.
Core product promise
CreatiQ should help a creator:
Add deals in under 1 minute.
See total earnings, pending payments, and average deal value instantly.
Track brand payments across platforms.
Export tax-ready CSVs in one click.
Understand top brands, top platforms, and payment patterns.
This kind of focused MVP is more likely to ship successfully than a broad, all-in-one creator-finance platform �.
Required platform
Please build this as a responsive web application, mobile-first, with clean modern UI and fast performance. The app should work smoothly on desktop and mobile browsers because many creators will use it from their phones �.
Core user flow
The main flow should be:
User lands on marketing site / app homepage.
Clicks “Get Free Access.”
Signs up with email and magic link.
Completes onboarding.
Adds first deal.
Sees dashboard update immediately.
Updates payment status over time.
Exports CSV when needed.
Upgrades from free to paid when deal limit is reached.
MVP features to build
Please include the following in version 1.
1. Authentication
Email-based sign-up and login.
Magic link authentication preferred.
Secure session handling.
Logout flow.
Basic account page.
2. Onboarding
After signup, ask:
Name.
Creator type.
Platforms used.
Approx monthly earnings.
This onboarding should end with a prompt to add the first deal.
3. Deal management
Users must be able to:
Add a deal.
Edit a deal.
Delete a deal.
View all deals in a table/list.
Filter by platform, payment status, deal status, date range, and brand.
Search by brand name.
Each deal should include:
Brand name.
Amount.
Content/deal type, e.g. Reel, Post, Video, Story, Affiliate, UGC.
Platform, e.g. Instagram, YouTube, Direct, Affiliate.
Deal status, e.g. proposed, approved, completed, cancelled.
Payment status, e.g. pending, paid, overdue.
Date.
Notes.
4. Dashboard
Dashboard should show:
Total earnings this month.
Total paid this month.
Total pending this month.
Number of deals this month.
Average deal value.
Recent deals.
Charts/visuals should include:
Earnings by platform.
Earnings trend over time.
5. Insights / analytics
Paid users should have an Insights page that shows:
Top brands by earnings.
Platform performance.
Payment analysis, including average time to payment if possible.
Content-type performance, e.g. reels vs posts vs videos.
Analytics should stay simple and actionable, because MVP scopes work best when tied to user value instead of overbuilt reporting �.
6. CSV export
Users should be able to export their deals to CSV. Export fields should include:
Brand.
Amount.
Type.
Platform.
Deal status.
Payment status.
Date.
Notes.
The export must be clean and accountant-friendly, since tax-ready exports are a core part of the value proposition �.
7. Pricing and limits
Free plan:
Up to 5 deals.
Basic dashboard stats only.
No charts.
No CSV export.
No advanced analytics.
Paid plan:
₹99/month.
Unlimited deals.
Full dashboard.
Charts.
CSV export.
Full analytics.
Email notifications.
Priority support.
Please build plan enforcement in the app.
8. Subscription billing
Need payment integration for paid plan. Since this is for India, developer should suggest the best payment option for recurring subscriptions, such as Razorpay or Stripe if suitable for this use case. The final recommendation should consider Indian billing realities and ease of implementation.
9. Notifications
Basic email notifications for:
Magic link login.
Upgrade confirmation.
Payment success/failure.
Possibly reminder emails for pending payments later if easy to include.
10. Admin panel
Need a lightweight admin area where I can:
View users.
View subscriptions.
View total deals count.
See free vs paid users.
Disable abusive accounts if needed.
Pages/screens required
Please build these screens:
Landing page / homepage.
Sign up / login.
Onboarding.
Dashboard.
Add/edit deal modal or page.
Deals list page.
Insights page.
Billing / subscription page.
Account/settings page.
Admin panel.
Suggested tech stack
Open to recommendations, but I prefer a modern stack suitable for SaaS MVP delivery. A freelancer should propose the stack clearly, because scoping templates commonly include choices such as [login to view URL] plus a backend/database service and billing/auth tools �. A good option may be:
Frontend: Next.js.
Backend/API: [login to view URL] API routes or similar.
Database: PostgreSQL.
Auth: Supabase/[login to view URL] or equivalent.
Hosting: Vercel or similar.
Billing: Razorpay or Stripe.
Email: Resend, Postmark, or similar.
I want the freelancer to justify the stack and choose what is fastest and safest for MVP delivery �.
Design requirements
Clean, modern, minimal UI.
Mobile-first responsive design.
Simple onboarding.
Fast deal entry.
Dashboard should feel easy, not enterprise-heavy.
Product should look creator-friendly, not like accounting software.
India-focused feel where relevant, especially around currency and tax export language.
Performance and usability expectations
The app should:
Load fast.
Be easy for non-technical users.
Allow very quick data entry.
Work smoothly on mobile.
Have basic empty states, validation, and error handling.
Be production-ready, not just a rough prototype.
A proper freelancer brief should define the expected handover and quality bar, not just the feature list �.
Deliverables expected from freelancer
Please include all of the following:
Full source code.
Deployment of live production app.
Database schema and setup.
Responsive UI implementation.
Payment integration.
Email auth setup.
CSV export feature.
Admin panel.
Basic documentation for setup and future maintenance.
Handover of all credentials, repos, env variables, deployment accounts, and third-party service configs, because this is a key part of a clean SaaS project handoff �.
Timeline request
Please provide:
Estimated timeline for MVP.
Milestones by week.
What can be shipped in phase 1 versus phase 2.
Most MVP scoping guidance recommends defining release boundaries clearly before development starts so the project stays focused �.
Proposal requirements for freelancers
When applying, please include:
Similar SaaS or dashboard apps you have built.
Stack you recommend and why.
Estimated timeline.
Fixed price or milestone-based quote.
What is included in MVP and what you would exclude.
Experience with auth, subscriptions, dashboards, and CSV exports.
Whether you can also handle UI/UX design.
Nice-to-have features
These are not mandatory for the first version, but mention if you can add them later:
Payment reminders to creators.
Recurring affiliate income tracking.
Multi-currency support.
GST-specific export improvements.
Advanced analytics.
Brand payment-speed insights.
PWA/mobile app wrapper.
CA/accountant sharing mode
description_snippet: Project title Build CreatiQ — a web app for Indian creators to track brand deals, earnings, payment status, and export tax-ready CSVs. Project overview CreatiQ is a SaaS web app for Indian creators who earn from multiple income sources such as Instag

title: قصيدة حب حرة ساحرة
skills: CONTENT WRITING | Content Writing | Poetry
full_description:
أكتب الآن مخطَّط قصيدة بعنوان «همسة منتصف الليل» وأرغب في تحويله إلى قصيدة شعرية متكاملة بالأسلوب الحر. الجو العام يدور حول الحب والرومانسية كما يظهر في النص المرفق: المطر على الزجاج، الهمس بالاسم، اللقاء العابر في المقهى، والدهشة التي تقلب موازين الأيام. أحتاج إلى قصيدة تنقل هذا البُعد الحميمي بلغة عربية رشيقة، مشحونة بالصور والرموز، ومن دون التقيد بالوزن العمودي أو القافية التقليدية.

القصيدة المنشودة
• نوع النص: قصيدة شعرية 
• النمط: حر بالكامل، مع انسياب موسيقي داخلي 
• الموضوع: الحب والرومانسية الهادئة التي تولد من تفاصيل صغيرة (قطرات المطر، ابتسامة، نظرة)

قبول العمل يكون عند استلام:
1. قصيدة أصلية خالية من أي اقتباس مباشر يزيد على سطرين متتاليين. 
2. طول تقريبي بين 20 – 30 سطراً يظل وفيّاً لروح «همسة منتصف الليل». 
3. لغة عاطفية صادقة، صور جديدة غير متكلَّفة، وتدرّج واضح من الهمس الأول حتى ذروة الاعتراف بالحب. 
4. ملف بصيغة DOCX أو TXT مع العنوان في الأعلى، متبوعاً بالقصيدة خالية من الملاحظات التحريرية.

بمجرّد تسليمي النص بالمعايير السابقة أستطيع اعتمادك فوراً وإغلاق المشروع.

Milestones should be realistic and I’m happy to review and sign off each one promptly. Any bugs discovered during acceptance testing are to be fixed before final hand-off at no extra cost.

• Full Flutter source code in a private Git repository 
• Release-ready builds for iOS (IPA) and Android (AAB) 
• Basic CI workflow (GitHub Actions or similar) compiling, running tests and generating artefacts 
• Post-launch support for minor revisions and hot-fixes during an agreed grace period

Once these items are met and the app passes my test scenarios—no crashes, no major UI glitches and successful end-to-end message delivery—I’ll consider the project complete and release the final milestone.

title: Bring My Artwork to Serene Animation
skills: 2D ANIMATION | 2D Animation | Character Animation | Video Production | Adobe Animate | Animation | Illustration | Caricature & Cartoons | 3D Animation | AI Art Creation
full_description:
Hi! I have an illustration that I'd like to turn into a subtle looping animation.
The main idea is not to make it look like a fully animated cartoon, but to make it feel like the artwork has quietly come alive. I want someone to look at it for a second and then slowly realize that things are gently moving.
The overall feeling should be calm, dreamy and intimate. Think of it as a painting breathing rather than a character acting.
I'd love to have a soft breeze running through the artwork. Small details like a few loose hair strands, the edge of the scarf, the bouquet wrapping paper and the flowers can move naturally with the wind. Nothing exaggerated.
The butterfly should be the main animated element. It can slowly open its wings, pause for a moment, take off, make a graceful circle around the composition, briefly land on one of the flowers, and then return to where it started. The movement should feel light and elegant, not fast or playful.
The character should also have a very subtle breathing motion, almost unnoticeable, just enough to make the illustration feel alive. A natural blink every now and then would be great as well.
I'd also like tiny details that add life without drawing attention to themselves, like a few floating pollen or dust particles, very gentle shifts in warm light across the artwork, and maybe a soft shadow from the butterfly while it's flying.
The camera doesn't need to move much. A very slow push-in or slight parallax is enough. No dramatic zooms, shakes or flashy transitions.
The most important thing is to preserve the original artwork completely. I don't want the illustration style to change, no morphing, no AI-style distortions, and no over-animation. Everything should stay true to the original image.
The animation should loop seamlessly, around 20–30 seconds, so someone could watch it for a while without noticing where it starts or ends.
Deliverables
4K master.
1080×1920 (vertical) version for Reels/Shorts.
1920×1080 (landscape) version for YouTube.

title: Automotive Grille Badge Design in Fusion 360
skills: FUSION 360 | Fusion 360 | 3D Printing | Manufacturing Design | Automotive Engineering | 3D Modelling | 3D Rendering | CAD/CAM | Graphic Design
full_description:
Fusion 360 Expert Needed – Production Ready Automotive Grille Badge

Project Description

I am looking for an experienced Fusion 360 designer with automotive product design experience to recreate a custom grille badge from the attached reference image.

The final model must be fully parametric, professionally engineered, and production-ready—not just a visual model.

This project is for an automotive aftermarket company, and the selected freelancer will have the opportunity to work on many future products.

Project Requirements

• Recreate the attached grille badge accurately
• Model the complete outer frame
• Create the internal honeycomb/checker pattern
• Model the center logo area as a separate component
• Design all rear mounting clips and attachment points
• Maintain OEM-style proportions and smooth surfaces
• Fully parametric Fusion 360 design
• Clean sketches and timeline
• Proper constraints and dimensions
• Manufacturing-ready design

Deliverables

• Fusion 360 (.f3d) source file
• STEP (.step)
• STL (.stl)
• Separate editable bodies/components
• Fully editable sketches
• Production-ready CAD model

Important

This is NOT simply a rendering project.

The design must be engineered for:
• 3D printing
• Injection molding
• Future LED integration
• Automotive outdoor use

Skills Required

• Fusion 360 Expert
• Mechanical CAD
• Automotive Product Design
• Industrial Design
• Product Engineering
• 3D Printing
• Injection Mold Design (preferred)

Please Include

• Fixed price
• Estimated completion time
• Portfolio of similar Fusion 360 or automotive projects
• Examples of production-ready CAD work

Long-Term Opportunity

This is the first project for a new automotive accessories brand. Successful completion will lead to ongoing work designing:

• Illuminated BMW grille badges
• LED license plate frames
• Interior ambient lighting products
• Mirror accessories
• Custom automotive trim pieces
• Additional OEM-style accessories

The attached reference image includes calibration dimensions. The final design should closely match the reference while remaining fully editable, parametric, and engineered for manufacturing.
description_snippet: Fusion 360 Expert Needed – Production Ready Automotive Grille Badge Project Description I am looking for an experienced Fusion 360 designer with automotive product design experience to recreate a custom grille badge from the attached reference image.

title: Hotel Electrical & Plumbing Design in Fiji use AS code
skills: REVIT | Revit | Building Design | Construction Management | Fire Alarm Design | CAD/CAM | AutoCAD | Electrical Engineering | PCB Layout
full_description:
I’m developing a new hotel in Fiji and need a designer who can produce coordinated electrical and plumbing plans that comply fully with Australian Standards (AS). Your work will guide local contractors, so accuracy and constructability are critical.

Scope of work
• Electrical systems: lighting layouts, power distribution schematics, and an integrated fire-alarm design. 
• Plumbing systems: cold and hot water reticulation, sanitary drainage, and any required pump or tank sizing. 
• All drawings, load and flow calculations, single-line diagrams, schedules, and material specifications delivered in CAD/Revit (DWG and PDF).

Key expectations
– Every element must reference the relevant AS clause. 
– Designs should accommodate Fiji’s supply voltages and local installation practices while remaining AS-compliant. 
– Coordinate fixture locations across disciplines and allow for hotel aesthetics and maintenance access. 
– Be available for one round of revisions after the initial review.

If you have recent hotel or hospitality experience and can start soon, I’d like to see examples of similar AS-based projects in your portfolio.
description_snippet: I’m developing a new hotel in Fiji and need a designer who can produce coordinated electrical and plumbing plans that comply fully with Australian Standards (AS). Your work will guide local contractors, so accuracy and constructability are critical.

title: On-Site Document Verification France

full_description:
1- Task is to visit an Address : Rue du Grand But, 59400 Lille, France
physically and take atleast 10-15 geotagged photos of the name board of the institutes captured from outside the building and preferably inside too. Download app for geotagged pics from the link: [login to view URL]

2- Get the verification forms filled, signed and stamped by the HR or relevant authority of these locations.

3- Get the details of verifier that is their name, designation, Department and Contact.

4- In case of refusal to fill form, take the written refusal statement with stamp or sign as a proof. If they don't give written, ask them that you want to record them saying their reason and take their details like name designation contact etc. If both isn't possible, just take their details like name of person whom you met, their designation, contact, or their visiting card.

5- If locations is not found on given address, then take 7 to 8 geotagged photos of exact address with right left name boards and make a small few seconds video of address. Also get the details of any nearby business (name of person, business name and contact who can tell you whether the institution existed or not)

6- Please ensure that the name on the document matches the name on the signboard of the authority you visit. If there is a different name, ask them for the reason for the discrepancy. ask them to write one line explanation on the form or provide BRC.

7- Check the forms before leaving the site, ensure that the start date, end date, and designation are the same on both the form and the certificate.

8- Check all the fields are properly filled on the form.

9- Keep in touch with me during the visit.
